sub_criteria.name: Common Characteristics of Good Applications
sub_criteria.definition: The application demonstrates characteristics commonly found in strong grant applications.

signals.signal_text: Clear research vision and career trajectory
Distinctive ideas with an independent direction
Well-justified training plan
Strong mentorship and institutional support

sub_criteria.pros: The application describes a research team with a mix of clinical and research expertise (g.1.b) and mentions successful NIHR grant applications and publications (g.1.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement of the lead applicant's personal research vision or career trajectory (g.1.a). No training plan is described (g.1.c). Mentorship is implied through team roles but not explicitly detailed as a support structure for the applicant (g.1.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement of the lead applicant's personal research vision or career trajectory (g.1.a). No training plan is described (g.1.c). Mentorship is implied through team roles but not explicitly detailed as a support structure for the applicant (g.1.d).

sub_criteria.name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award
sub_criteria.definition: The applicant clearly articulates why this specific award is the right next step in their career.
sub_criteria.group_name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award

signals.signal_text: Explain why this award is the right next step in your career (not just another project grant)
What difference would securing an award make to your career, and why now?
How will an award build on your existing experience and what new skills will it enable you to gain?
How will it develop your research partnerships within the NHS, public health or social care, charity organisations, industry or other organisations?

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text focuses entirely on the research project and team composition. It lacks any personal narrative explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant, the difference it would make to their career, or how it builds on their specific experience (g.2.a, g.2.b, g.2.c). There is no discussion of how the award will develop the applicant's specific research partnerships (g.2.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text focuses entirely on the research project and team composition. It lacks any personal narrative explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant, the difference it would make to their career, or how it builds on their specific experience (g.2.a, g.2.b, g.2.c). There is no discussion of how the award will develop the applicant's specific research partnerships (g.2.d).

sub_criteria.name: Relevant Research Experience and Training
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates prior research experience and/or training that prepares the applicant to successfully undertake the proposed award in applied health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of previous projects, research methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study
Evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications
Alignment between prior experience and skills required for the proposed project

sub_criteria.pros: The text explicitly lists team members' prior experience with electronic health records, scoping reviews, and NIHR-funded projects, which aligns well with the proposed methods (g.3.a, g.3.c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While experience is mentioned, there is little evidence of formal training courses or certifications for the applicant specifically (g.3.b). The evidence is largely about the team rather than the individual applicant's specific training history.
sub_criteria.rationale: While experience is mentioned, there is little evidence of formal training courses or certifications for the applicant specifically (g.3.b). The evidence is largely about the team rather than the individual applicant's specific training history.

sub_criteria.name: Impactful Research Outputs and Outcomes
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful research outputs or outcomes appropriate to the applicant’s career stage, demonstrating contribution to the field.

signals.signal_text: Number and quality of publications, reports, or presentations
Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: The application mentions a track record of publications and successful NIHR grant applications for key team members (g.4.a, g.4.c). It implies practical impact through the goal of improving care for PLWHIV (g.4.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No specific citation metrics or indicators of impact are provided (g.4.b). The description of practical impact is general and tied to the project goals rather than specific outcomes of past work (g.4.d). The evidence is team-focused, not applicant-specific.
sub_criteria.rationale: No specific citation metrics or indicators of impact are provided (g.4.b). The description of practical impact is general and tied to the project goals rather than specific outcomes of past work (g.4.d). The evidence is team-focused, not applicant-specific.

sub_criteria.name: Trajectory Towards Research Leadership
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates potential and progression towards becoming a future leader in health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Statements of career ambitions and long-term goals
Evidence of increasing responsibility in previous roles (e.g., leading projects, mentoring)
Participation in leadership or professional development programs
Recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential

sub_criteria.pros: The applicant is listed as a joint lead, suggesting some level of responsibility (g.5.b). Team members are involved in external relations committees, suggesting peer recognition (g.5.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There are no explicit statements of the applicant's career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). No evidence of participation in leadership or professional development programs is provided (g.5.c). The evidence of increasing responsibility is weak and inferred from the team list rather than a personal narrative.
sub_criteria.rationale: There are no explicit statements of the applicant's career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). No evidence of participation in leadership or professional development programs is provided (g.5.c). The evidence of increasing responsibility is weak and inferred from the team list rather than a personal narrative.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Practitioner-Academic Career (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: For applicants including a clinical or practice element, shows a clear commitment to combining research with professional practice.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions
Involvement in clinical or practice-based research
Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities
Participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications

sub_criteria.pros: The research team includes GP academics and HIV specialists, demonstrating strong involvement in clinical and practice-based research (g.6.b). Team members are part of professional networks like BHIVA (g.6.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement from the applicant regarding their intentions to pursue a practitioner-academic career (g.6.a). No evidence is provided regarding how the applicant balances practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement from the applicant regarding their intentions to pursue a practitioner-academic career (g.6.a). No evidence is provided regarding how the applicant balances practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c).

sub_criteria.name: Quality of the Plain English Summary
sub_criteria.definition: Clear, simple description of the research that a non-specialist audience can understand; explains problem, importance, plan, beneficiaries, and real-world use; avoids jargon; aligns with the detailed proposal.

signals.signal_text: Readability scores (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid)
Low density of unexplained jargon or technical terms
Presence of problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact
Alignment with detailed proposal content
Sentence structure and coherence for non-specialist understanding
Emphasis on societal or patient relevance

sub_criteria.pros: The Plain English Summary is highly readable (Flesch-Kincaid grade 5.3, reading ease 74.35) with very low jargon density (2.41%). It clearly articulates the problem (ageing PLWHIV, lack of GP guidance), aims, methods (three-part study), beneficiaries, and real-world impact (longer, healthier lives). The content aligns perfectly with the Detailed Research Plan, covering all required elements in accessible language.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: None identified; the summary is concise, coherent, and fully meets the criteria for a non-specialist audience.
sub_criteria.rationale: None identified; the summary is concise, coherent, and fully meets the criteria for a non-specialist audience.

sub_criteria.name: Evidence Review Quality & Relevance
sub_criteria.definition: How comprehensively and critically the application reviews existing literature and evidence.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of references cited
Presence of critical analysis vs. mere summary
Identification of knowledge gaps
Coherence and logical flow

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides a critical review of existing evidence, citing specific reports (e.g., BHIVA 2017) and identifying limitations (dated research, poor quality evidence for shared-care). It clearly identifies knowledge gaps regarding GP guidance and PLWHIV experiences, justifying the need for an updated scoping review.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the review is strong, the specific list of references is embedded in the text rather than presented as a formal bibliography, which slightly limits the immediate assessment of the breadth of citations, though the content is robust.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the review is strong, the specific list of references is embedded in the text rather than presented as a formal bibliography, which slightly limits the immediate assessment of the breadth of citations, though the content is robust.

sub_criteria.name: Research Scope & Award Appropriateness
sub_criteria.definition: Whether the project scope matches the scale and expectations of the proposed award (doctoral, postdoctoral, etc.).

signals.signal_text: Alignment of aims with award level
Feasibility indicators (time, complexity, tasks)
Proportion of work relative to award expectations

sub_criteria.pros: The project scope is well-defined with three distinct workstreams (scoping review, database analysis, co-design) and clear timelines (months 1-16). The 18-month duration and £180k budget align well with the complexity of the tasks, demonstrating high feasibility and appropriate scale for the award.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the scope is realistic and well-matched to the resources and timeframe.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the scope is realistic and well-matched to the resources and timeframe.

sub_criteria.name: Research Design & Methodological Rigor
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity, appropriateness, and robustness of study design to answer the research questions.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of methods
Logical alignment between aims and methods
Discussion of controls, sample size, or methodology justification
Structured methodological sections

sub_criteria.pros: Methods are explicitly described with high rigor: Arksey and O'Malley framework for the scoping review, CPRD Aurum database for quantitative analysis with specific inclusion criteria, and a structured Person-Based Approach for co-design. The alignment between aims and methods is logical and robust.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The sample size justification for the CPRD analysis notes that HIV is uncommon and a minimum sample size is difficult to prescribe, relying on a previous cohort of ~3,000. While explained, this is a minor limitation in statistical power planning compared to a calculated power analysis.
sub_criteria.rationale: The sample size justification for the CPRD analysis notes that HIV is uncommon and a minimum sample size is difficult to prescribe, relying on a previous cohort of ~3,000. While explained, this is a minor limitation in statistical power planning compared to a calculated power analysis.

sub_criteria.name: Potential Impact on Patients, Public, and Health Services
sub_criteria.definition: Likely benefits or influence of the research on patients, carers, or health and care systems.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of direct or indirect patient/public benefits
Alignment with health priorities or societal needs
Clarity and specificity of expected outcomes

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly articulates benefits for PLWHIV (longer, healthier lives) and the NHS (effective resource use). It aligns with national priorities (NHS Long Term Plan) and specifies expected outcomes: evidence-based clinical guidance and a co-designed care model ready for piloting.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: None identified; the impact pathway is clear and directly linked to patient and system benefits.
sub_criteria.rationale: None identified; the impact pathway is clear and directly linked to patient and system benefits.

sub_criteria.name: Engagement with People & Communities
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful involvement of patients, carers, or communities.

signals.signal_text: Use of co-design, participatory methods, or consultations
References to ethical considerations and feedback mechanisms
Description of involvement at multiple project stages

sub_criteria.pros: Strong evidence of meaningful engagement: PLWHIV are co-applicants and co-lead the stakeholder group. The Person-Based Approach is used throughout all workstreams, with specific plans for focus groups, feedback loops, and compensation for participants. Involvement spans design, review, and dissemination.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: None identified; the engagement plan is comprehensive and integrated into the research lifecycle.
sub_criteria.rationale: None identified; the engagement plan is comprehensive and integrated into the research lifecycle.

sub_criteria.name: Justification of Resources & Award Costs
sub_criteria.definition: Appropriateness and clarity of requested resources for the planned work.

signals.signal_text: Explicit links between tasks and resources requested
Budget proportionality to project scope
Clear rationale for funding level

sub_criteria.pros: The total cost (£180,401.32) and duration (18 months) are clearly stated. The application mentions that compensation for stakeholder time and expenses is costed in the budget, linking resources to specific engagement activities.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text does not provide a detailed breakdown of the budget (e.g., staff costs vs. overheads vs. consumables) or explicit justification for the specific funding level relative to the tasks, limiting the ability to fully assess resource appropriateness beyond the total figure.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text does not provide a detailed breakdown of the budget (e.g., staff costs vs. overheads vs. consumables) or explicit justification for the specific funding level relative to the tasks, limiting the ability to fully assess resource appropriateness beyond the total figure.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of Previous Research (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: Prior relevant research experience demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed project.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of publications or outputs
Evidence of prior successful projects
Skills alignment with proposed work

sub_criteria.pros: The research team demonstrates strong prior experience: joint leads and co-applicants have track records of successful NIHR grants, publications in HIV care, and expertise in electronic health records and scoping reviews. Skills are explicitly aligned with the proposed work (e.g., MTR for statistics, SD for PBA).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the team's collective experience is strong, the specific prior outputs of the lead applicant (Dr. Richard Ma) are mentioned generally (PhD research, NIHR fellowship) without a detailed list of specific publications or projects led solely by him, though the team context is robust.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the team's collective experience is strong, the specific prior outputs of the lead applicant (Dr. Richard Ma) are mentioned generally (PhD research, NIHR fellowship) without a detailed list of specific publications or projects led solely by him, though the team context is robust.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly identifies underrepresented groups (trans, Black African, rural) and plans 'extra efforts' to ensure their representation in focus groups and the stakeholder group. It acknowledges intersectionality (stigma, racism, sexism) and aims to address barriers to participation.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While strategies for recruitment are mentioned, specific adaptive methods for accessibility (e.g., specific formats for disabled participants beyond general 'online' options) are not detailed in depth, though the general commitment is strong.
sub_criteria.rationale: While strategies for recruitment are mentioned, specific adaptive methods for accessibility (e.g., specific formats for disabled participants beyond general 'online' options) are not detailed in depth, though the general commitment is strong.

sub_criteria.name: Quality and Relevance of Proposed Training
sub_criteria.definition: How well-structured, clear, and relevant the training plan is in relation to the project’s goals and the applicant’s research needs.

signals.signal_text: Completeness and clarity of training description
Alignment of training topics with project methods or techniques
Structured timeline or sequencing of training activities
Explicit links between training content and skill acquisition

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided application text or belief state regarding a specific training plan.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.1' as a missing signal. There is no description of training activities, timelines, or content.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.1' as a missing signal. There is no description of training activities, timelines, or content.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability to Personal Development and Project Needs
sub_criteria.definition: How well the proposed training addresses the applicant’s current skills gaps and supports successful project delivery.

signals.signal_text: Identification of personal skill gaps
Clear mapping between training activities and project tasks
Evidence that proposed training is targeted rather than generic
Mention of mentorship, workshops, or formal courses relevant to development needs

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided application text or belief state regarding personal development needs or skill gaps.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.2' as a missing signal. There is no identification of skill gaps or mapping of training to project tasks.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.2' as a missing signal. There is no identification of skill gaps or mapping of training to project tasks.

sub_criteria.name: Support for Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: The extent to which the training plan, and any professional practice components, contribute to the applicant’s long-term career goals.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of career goals
Connections between training activities and future career trajectory
Indications of skill transferability beyond the current project
Reference to professional practice or experiential learning where applicable

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided application text or belief state regarding career aspirations linked to training.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.3' as a missing signal. There is no statement of career goals or connection between training and future trajectory.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. The belief state explicitly lists 'td.3' as a missing signal. There is no statement of career goals or connection between training and future trajectory.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record of the Contracting Organisation (and Partners)
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrated expertise, experience, and research achievements of the organisation(s) in the proposed research area.

signals.signal_text: Publication or project history in relevant research fields
Previous funding success or awards in similar domains
Mention of prior collaborations or partnerships
Highlighted achievements or notable outputs

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides strong evidence of the team's track record, explicitly mentioning 'successful NIHR grant applications' and 'track record of publications' for key members (JH, WL, JVR) in the relevant field of HIV care and general practice. It also highlights specific prior outputs like a PhD funded by NIHR and published work on PBA and scoping reviews.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the team's individual track records are well-documented, the specific institutional track record of the 'Integrated Care Board' (the contracting organisation) is not detailed beyond its role as the host. The evidence focuses heavily on the researchers rather than the organisation's broader research achievements.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the team's individual track records are well-documented, the specific institutional track record of the 'Integrated Care Board' (the contracting organisation) is not detailed beyond its role as the host. The evidence focuses heavily on the researchers rather than the organisation's broader research achievements.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability of Supervisory Support and Mentorship Arrangements
sub_criteria.definition: Quality, clarity, and appropriateness of supervision and mentoring for supporting the applicant’s research and career development.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of supervisor roles and responsibilities
Evidence of mentorship plans, meetings, or training support
Alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs
Presence of structured supervision framework (frequency, format, accountability)

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly defines roles for methodological and statistical advice (MTR), PPI training (SD, ANe), and co-leadership of stakeholder groups. It explicitly states that training on PBA will be provided and that the stakeholder group will meet every four months, demonstrating a structured support framework aligned with project needs.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the support structure is robust, the specific frequency or format of direct academic supervision for the applicant (e.g., weekly meetings, formal review cycles) is not explicitly detailed, relying more on the description of project management and stakeholder engagement structures.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the support structure is robust, the specific frequency or format of direct academic supervision for the applicant (e.g., weekly meetings, formal review cycles) is not explicitly detailed, relying more on the description of project management and stakeholder engagement structures.

sub_criteria.name: Appropriateness of the Contracting Organisation to Support the Award and Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: How well the organisation (and any partners) can provide the infrastructure, resources, and environment necessary to deliver the project and support the applicant’s career growth.

signals.signal_text: Description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided
Links between organisational support and project success
Alignment of organisational focus with applicant’s career trajectory
Evidence of tailored support for award type (doctoral, postdoctoral)

sub_criteria.pros: The application identifies the contracting organisation as an Integrated Care Board, which implies access to NHS networks and primary care clinicians for recruitment. The team composition includes GP academics who provide access to these networks, linking organisational support to project success.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is very limited specific description of the facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the Integrated Care Board itself. The evidence for support is largely inferred from the team's existing networks rather than explicit statements about the organisation's capacity to support the award or the applicant's specific career trajectory.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is very limited specific description of the facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the Integrated Care Board itself. The evidence for support is largely inferred from the team's existing networks rather than explicit statements about the organisation's capacity to support the award or the applicant's specific career trajectory.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Inclusive and Supportive Research Culture
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that the organisation fosters inclusivity, research integrity, and a supportive environment for all researchers.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion
Evidence of inclusive recruitment, mentoring, or support initiatives
References to adherence to research integrity principles
Statements on organisational culture, training, or initiatives supporting underrepresented groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates a strong commitment to inclusivity through specific strategies to recruit underrepresented groups (trans, Black African, rural) and ensuring the stakeholder group reflects demographics most affected by HIV. It explicitly mentions compensation for time and expenses, supporting an equitable environment.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The evidence for an inclusive culture is primarily project-specific (recruitment strategies) rather than describing broader organisational policies or initiatives. There is no explicit mention of the organisation's adherence to research integrity principles or specific institutional training programs for underrepresented groups beyond the project scope.
sub_criteria.rationale: The evidence for an inclusive culture is primarily project-specific (recruitment strategies) rather than describing broader organisational policies or initiatives. There is no explicit mention of the organisation's adherence to research integrity principles or specific institutional training programs for underrepresented groups beyond the project scope.

sub_criteria.name: Integration of Lived Experience in Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: How input from patients, carers, or people with relevant lived experience has shaped the research questions, design, and methods.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of consultation or co-design activities
Specific examples of changes made to study design based on lived experience
References to patient or community advisory groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly details the use of the Person-Based Approach (PBA) with PLWHIV as co-applicants leading a stakeholder group to co-design the care model. It describes specific activities like focus groups and feedback loops where the model is refined based on input.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan for co-design is strong, the application describes the *process* of making changes rather than providing specific *examples* of changes already made to the design based on prior lived experience input (as this is a proposal for future work). This prevents a perfect score on wp.1.b.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan for co-design is strong, the application describes the *process* of making changes rather than providing specific *examples* of changes already made to the design based on prior lived experience input (as this is a proposal for future work). This prevents a perfect score on wp.1.b.

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusivity of the Research Team
sub_criteria.definition: Extent to which the research team reflects diversity in terms of skills, experience, and demographic representation.

signals.signal_text: Explicit mentions of team composition across gender, ethnicity, age, or other relevant characteristics
Statements about inclusion in hiring or team-building
Evidence of equitable task allocation and collaboration

sub_criteria.pros: The research team includes co-applicants with lived experience of HIV (ANa, THI) and a mix of clinical and research expertise. The team structure explicitly integrates these diverse perspectives into the leadership.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit mention of the team's demographic composition (gender, ethnicity, age) beyond the lived experience of HIV. There are no specific statements about inclusion in hiring practices or detailed evidence of equitable task allocation beyond the general role descriptions.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit mention of the team's demographic composition (gender, ethnicity, age) beyond the lived experience of HIV. There are no specific statements about inclusion in hiring practices or detailed evidence of equitable task allocation beyond the general role descriptions.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Maximising Public and Patient Benefit
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity and strength of the plan to ensure research outcomes meaningfully benefit patients, carers, and the public.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of dissemination, knowledge translation, or implementation plans
Clear links between study objectives and anticipated benefits
Emphasis on actionable outcomes or improvements in health/care

sub_criteria.pros: A comprehensive dissemination plan is outlined, targeting professionals, policymakers, and the public via journals, conferences, social media, and press releases. The application clearly links the study objectives to anticipated benefits: improved health, longer lives, and evidence-based clinical guidance for PLWHIV.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan is strong, the specific mechanisms for translating the co-designed model into immediate practice changes (beyond the future pilot study) are described as a future step, slightly limiting the immediacy of the actionable outcomes.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan is strong, the specific mechanisms for translating the co-designed model into immediate practice changes (beyond the future pilot study) are described as a future step, slightly limiting the immediacy of the actionable outcomes.

sub_criteria.name: Representation of Seldom Heard or Underserved Groups
sub_criteria.definition: Degree to which participants and PPI contributors represent groups historically underrepresented in research, including those facing health inequalities.

signals.signal_text: Mention of specific demographic groups (age, race, ethnicity, disability, gender, sexual orientation, religion/belief, location, socioeconomic status)
Evidence of targeted recruitment strategies
Discussion of barriers to participation and mitigation strategies

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly identifies specific seldom-heard groups: men who have sex with men, Black African people, trans and gender diverse individuals, and those in non-metropolitan areas. It states a commitment to ensuring the stakeholder group reflects these demographics and plans 'extra efforts' to recruit them for focus groups.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While barriers like stigma and racism are acknowledged, the specific mitigation strategies for overcoming these barriers during recruitment (beyond 'extra efforts') are not detailed in depth.
sub_criteria.rationale: While barriers like stigma and racism are acknowledged, the specific mitigation strategies for overcoming these barriers during recruitment (beyond 'extra efforts') are not detailed in depth.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusivity Across the Research Lifecycle
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstration that inclusion is considered at all stages of the project: planning, recruitment, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

signals.signal_text: References to inclusion in study design, participant selection, and analysis
Consideration of accessibility and equity in all project stages
Documented plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation

sub_criteria.pros: Inclusion is embedded across the entire lifecycle: PPI advisors help write the proposal, co-applicants lead the stakeholder group for co-design, and focus groups are used for refinement. The PBA framework ensures views are systematically recorded and acted upon.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The plan mentions ongoing evaluation and adaptation of the model, but specific metrics or documented plans for evaluating the *inclusivity* of the process itself (beyond the model refinement) are not explicitly detailed.
sub_criteria.rationale: The plan mentions ongoing evaluation and adaptation of the model, but specific metrics or documented plans for evaluating the *inclusivity* of the process itself (beyond the model refinement) are not explicitly detailed.

sub_criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly states that compensation for time and expenses for the stakeholder group and focus group participants are costed in the budget. It also mentions training on PBA will be provided.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the text confirms costs are included, it does not provide specific budget line items or amounts for these inclusion activities, relying on the statement that they are 'costed in the budget' rather than showing the breakdown.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the text confirms costs are included, it does not provide specific budget line items or amounts for these inclusion activities, relying on the statement that they are 'costed in the budget' rather than showing the breakdown.

sub_criteria.name: Use of Formatting, Headings, and Subheadings
sub_criteria.definition: Effective use of text formatting to make the application clear, navigable, and easy to read.

signals.signal_text: Presence and consistency of headings and subheadings
Use of bold or emphasis for key terms
Structured hierarchy of sections (logical nesting of headings)
Patterns detected via text parsing for formatting markers

sub_criteria.pros: The application form analysis confirms the presence of 10 numbered heading-like lines and 22 bullet/numbered list markers, indicating strong use of headings and subheadings. The section order follows a logical hierarchy (context, summary, plan, PPI, training, budget). Extracted emphasis markers (13) are present, though the parser notes potential loss of bold formatting during extraction.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While emphasis markers are detected, the parser limitation note suggests that specific bold/emphasis formatting may have been lost during text extraction, preventing a perfect score for signal af.1.b despite the presence of markers.
sub_criteria.rationale: While emphasis markers are detected, the parser limitation note suggests that specific bold/emphasis formatting may have been lost during text extraction, preventing a perfect score for signal af.1.b despite the presence of markers.

sub_criteria.name: Limited Duplication and Repetition
sub_criteria.definition: Avoidance of unnecessary repetition within or across sections.

signals.signal_text: Semantic similarity measures between sentences/paragraphs
Detection of repeated phrases or ideas
Novelty score for each section (unique content vs. overlap)

sub_criteria.pros: The analysis explicitly reports a duplicate sentence rate of 0.0 and a repeated line rate of 0.0. No high cross-section lexical overlap was detected at the 0.18 threshold, indicating excellent avoidance of unnecessary repetition and high novelty across sections.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No drawbacks identified; the metrics for duplication and repetition are optimal.
sub_criteria.rationale: No drawbacks identified; the metrics for duplication and repetition are optimal.

sub_criteria.name: Logical Flow and Coherence Across Sections
sub_criteria.definition: The application progresses logically, with a clear thread linking the research question, objectives, and work packages.

signals.signal_text: Semantic coherence between sections (using embeddings or similarity metrics)
Explicit linking of objectives to methods and work packages
Presence of transitional phrases or linking statements
Detectable narrative thread anchored on research aims

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates logical flow with 7 transition phrases and 42 objective-method-budget link terms. The section order moves logically from applicant context through the research plan to budget, establishing a clear narrative thread.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the structural indicators (transition phrases, link terms) are strong, the analysis relies on parser-derived counts rather than deep semantic embedding analysis of the narrative thread itself, leaving a minor gap in confirming the depth of semantic coherence across all sections.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the structural indicators (transition phrases, link terms) are strong, the analysis relies on parser-derived counts rather than deep semantic embedding analysis of the narrative thread itself, leaving a minor gap in confirming the depth of semantic coherence across all sections.
